author: Austin Crawford
title: Crazy-as-a-Coot Bird Walk
summary: A twice-monthly bird walk, for birders walkers of all abilities. BI locations vary based on prevailing wind, weather conditions, and hot bird tips.
content:
A twice-monthly bird walk, for birders walkers of all abilities. BI locations vary based on prevailing wind, weather conditions, and hot bird tips.

 

Contact Kim for location: 401-595-7055
address: Block Island New Shoreham Washington County

author: Austin Crawford
title: Alzheimer's Information at Odd Fellows Cafe
summary: A representative from the RI chapter of the Alzheimer's Association will be on island this Wednesday, 11/06, at Odd Fellows Cafe from 9-1pm to share information and answer questions about this disease.
content: A representative from the RI chapter of the Alzheimer's Association will be on island this Wednesday, 11/06, at Odd Fellows Cafe from 9-1pm to share information and answer questions about this disease.

author: Austin Crawford
title: 4th Of July Celebration At Ballard’s!
summary: Join us for the ultimate 4th of July celebration at Ballard’s Beach Resort! Get ready for a day filled with incredible live music, beachside vibes, and non-stop entertainment. This is the event you don’t want to miss!
content:
Join us for the ultimate 4th of July celebration at Ballard’s Beach Resort! Get ready for a day filled with incredible live music, beachside vibes, and non-stop entertainment. This is the event you don’t want to miss!

 

Event Highlights:

 

 Live Music Lineup:

 

Bon Jovi Tribute Band (Don Jovi): Rock out with Don Jovi from 1:00 PM – 5:00 PM on our beach stage. Get ready to sing along to all your favorite Bon Jovi classics!

 

Def Leppard Tribute Band (Def Leppard Experience): Enjoy a powerful performance by Def Leppard Experience from 12:00 PM – 4:00 PM on our patio stage. Relive the magic of Def Leppard’s iconic hits!

 

DJ Dali: Keep the party going with DJ Dali spinning the hottest tracks from 4:00 PM – 6:30 PM on our patio stage. Dance the afternoon away!

 

Kevin Herchen: End the night with an unforgettable performance by Kevin Herchen from 6:00 PM – 10:00 PM. His soulful tunes and energetic style will make this 4th of July one to remember.

 

Ticket Details:

Price: $40 per person

Age Restriction: This event is 21+ only

Included: Access to all performances and stages

To purchase your tickets click the link above.  

See you there!

author: Austin Crawford
title: RI Hospitality Association Training Class  Essential Skills: Boost Your Service Game
summary: ESSENTIAL SKILLS: BOOST YOUR SERVICE GAME
content:
ESSENTIAL SKILLS: BOOST YOUR SERVICE GAME
Front of house staff are the face of your business. This 3-hour sales training covers giving
purpose and perspective, building knowledge and confidence, encouraging a positive work ethic, creating a team environment, serving better, making more money, and having fun doing it! Front of house staff are the face of your business.
 

TO REGISTER FOR BOOST YOUR SERVICE GAME, PLEASE CONTACT: emma@blockislandinfo.com | 401.466.5200 

When?
TUESDAY, JUNE 25TH | 9AM-12PM at The National Hotel

author: Austin Crawford
title: Bake, Book and Bloom
summary: Summer kicks off, at the Library, with the Bake, Book, and Bloom Sale, which will be held by the Friends of the Island Free Library on Saturday May 25th at 9 a.m. There will be the traditional offerings of baked goods, used books, and a front lawn filled with plants and flowers from Clark Farms.
content: Summer kicks off, at the Library, with the Bake, Book, and Bloom Sale, which will be held by the Friends of the Island Free Library on Saturday May 25th at 9 a.m. There will be the traditional offerings of baked goods, used books, and a front lawn filled with plants and flowers from Clark Farms.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Yoga & Osteoporosis Awareness at BI Community Center
summary: Join Susan Stover for a gentle yoga flow focused on balance and weight-bearing poses followed by an informative presentation by nurse practitioner Laurie Anderson highlighting osteoporosis risks, preventative measures, and treatments.
content:
Join Susan Stover for a gentle yoga flow focused on balance and weight-bearing poses followed by an informative presentation by nurse practitioner Laurie Anderson highlighting osteoporosis risks, preventative measures, and treatments.

Class will be from 9-10, with Laurie's presentation immediately following. Please bring a mat & blocks if you have them - we will have some to share! Light refreshments will be served.

author: Austin Crawford
title: St Patrick's Brunch at Saint Andrew Parish Center
summary: Join us for a delicious brunch of familiar favorites.
content:
Join us for a delicious brunch of familiar favorites.

$12.00 Adults

$6.00 Children

 

Corned beef hash, scrambled eggs, sausage, bacon, scalloped tomatoes, hash browns, Irish oatmeal, spinach quiche, fruit salad, fresh baked goods,

Irish soda bread coffee, tea, juices

author: BI Chamber
title: Block Island Film Festival
summary: The Block Island Film Festival boasts notable actors, directors, screenwriters, filmmakers, student filmmakers and entertainment professionals, on the beautiful resort destination known as Block Island, located 13 miles off the coast of Rhode Island. A consortium of global industry professionals gather to mingle with contemporaries, and the public, in intimate settings, such as film screenings, spotlight programs, tribute and award ceremonies, question and answer forums, and nightly social mixers.
content: The Block Island Film Festival boasts notable actors, directors, screenwriters, filmmakers, student filmmakers and entertainment professionals, on the beautiful resort destination known as Block Island, located 13 miles off the coast of Rhode Island. author: Austin Crawford
title: 2024 Block Island Triathlon
summary: The Island's largest and most popular race begins with a 1/4 mile open water ocean swim; followed by 12 miles of biking over rolling hills with breathtaking views of the island. The last stretch is a 4 mile run on beautiful Cresent Beach. Questions? Call Dave 401-466-3223. email: recreation@new-shoreham.com.
content:
The Island's largest and most popular race begins with a 1/4 mile open water ocean swim; followed by 12 miles of biking over rolling hills with breathtaking views of the island. The last stretch is a 4 mile run on beautiful Cresent Beach. Questions? Call Dave 401-466-3223. email: recreation@new-shoreham.com.

The race begins at 9:00 a.m. There is a 6:00am boat departing Point Judith race morning. If you aren't on the island Friday night, you will be required to take this boat. Please don't forget to buy a ticket for your bike. If you plan on being on island Friday night, make reservations now. If the ferry is canceled race day, or there is severe weather; the race will be canceled. Race fee is non-refundable. 

Roads are NOT closed during the bike portion.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Passport to the Arts Celebration
summary: Please join the Block Island Arts Council on Saturday October 7, 2023 from 9:00 am to 7:00 pm for our inaugural, island-wide Open Studio event. We will be hosting a family-friendly, interactive day of arts exploration will allow guests to meet dozens of Block Island artists from many different genres in over 30 different venues across the island.
content:
Please join the Block Island Arts Council on Saturday October 7, 2023 from 9:00 am to 7:00 pm for our inaugural, island-wide Open Studio event. We will be hosting a family-friendly, interactive day of arts exploration will allow guests to meet dozens of Block Island artists from many different genres in over 30 different venues across the island.

 

Begin your adventure traveling the island for a day of beautiful arts and crafts, exciting entertainment, and free hands-on art activities by picking up your free Passport to the Arts and Map/Guide between 9 and 11:30 a.m. at the Block Island Chamber of Commerce. Guests will collect Passport Stamps at each venue they visit during the day. At the end of the day visitors will receive a free raffle ticket for every stamp they’ve collected. Each member of your group or family should get their passport stamped at every venue you visit; you can each earn as many as 20 stamps through the day. You will receive one chance in our free raffle for each stamp you’ve earned. The more art you appreciate through your day, the more chances you will have to win a great prize!

 

At the end of your day, bring your stamped passport to raffle tables; at the B.I. Chamber of Commerce from 2:30 to 4:30 p.m.

 

Rain or shine, Winfield’s will host an array of artists from 9 am to 4 pm and live performances will be held on the stage at Yellow Kittens from 12 until 4 p.m.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Last Block Island Arts & Crafts Guild Fair
content: This will be the last Block Island Arts and Crafts Guild. The Block Island Arts and Crafts Guild is an outdoor market dedicated to the arts. Also, ten percent of all proceeds go towards scholarships for B.I. School students.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Last Farmers Market @ Historical Society
summary: This will be the last Farmers Market of the year. This Farmers Market will join the last Block Island Arts and Guild Fair. Locally grown fruits and vegetables, honey, shellfish, Photography, Pottery, Island made Arts and Crafts
content: This will be the last Farmers Market of the year. This Farmers Market will join the last Block Island Arts and Guild Fair. Locally grown fruits and vegetables, honey, shellfish, Photography, Pottery, Island made Arts and Crafts

author: Austin Crawford
title: Andy’s Way Bird Walk
summary: Meet in parking area
content:
Meet in parking area
A bird walk for all skill levels. Bring binoculars and wear shoes that can get wet. Time changes weekly based on the tide.

cherring@tnc.org

401-932-2941
address: Andy's Way New Shoreham Washington County

author: Austin Crawford
title: Marsh-Mucking for Kids
summary: All ages welcome to explore the salt marsh and to learn about the species found at Andy’s Way! (Kids must wear water shoes and parents must attend).
content:

author: Austin Crawford
title: Nature Walk & Community Science Osprey Monitoring
summary: Location TBD, Email kim.gaffett@tnc.org
content:
Location TBD, Email kim.gaffett@tnc.org
Block Island hosts many cool community science projects. Join the fun, different subject and location each week, and help collect important data about Block Island’s special critters and habitats.
